slides.title: Chronic stress may age you *down to the cell*.
slides.sub: The constant low-grade kind is the problem. You cannot avoid stress, but you can get better at recovering.

slides.title: Stress reaches your cells
slides.body: Long-term stress is *linked to faster cellular aging*, including shorter telomeres, the protective caps on your DNA.

slides.title: It's the slow burn
slides.body: It is the *constant low-grade kind* that does the damage, not the occasional rough week your body bounces back from.

slides.title: Down-regulate daily
slides.body: Lower it most days with *slow breathing, nature and movement*. Small resets keep stress from quietly stacking up.

slides.title: Sleep and people help
slides.body: Good sleep and *real connection buffer stress*. Both make hard days land softer and clear faster.

slides.title: Recover, don't avoid
slides.body: You cannot avoid stress, but you can *get better at recovering*. The goal is a quicker return to calm.

slides.title: Track the habits that *help you recover*.
slides.body: Vibe Health scores your sleep, food, water and steps, so the habits that help you bounce back show up in one number.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Chronic stress may age you down to the cell, with long-term stress linked to shorter telomeres. 🧘

It is the constant low-grade kind that does the damage. Down-regulate daily with slow breathing, nature and movement, and let sleep and connection buffer it. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns your recovery habits into one daily score → link in bio.
